name: Salman ibn Mudharib
title: Devoted Fellow Traveler
shortDescription: The cousin of Zuhayr ibn Qayn who joined Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan mid-journey and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Salman ibn Mudharib al-Bajali was a warrior from the Bajila tribe and the cousin of Zuhayr ibn Qayn. He was returning to Kufa from the Hajj pilgrimage along with Zuhayr ibn Qayn's caravan.

When Zuhayr was transformed by his wife's (Dulham) words and decided to rush to Imam Hussain's (AS) aid, Salman ibn Mudharib, who had a heart ready to accept the truth, did not leave his cousin alone and joined Imam Hussain's (AS) luminous camp with him.

Salman stood by the Imam throughout all the difficult days before Ashura. With the arrival of Ashura morning and the start of Umar Sa'd's brutal all-out attack (known as the first attack), Salman, like the other loyal companions of the Imam, bravely drew his sword.

After a courageous battle against the flood of enemy soldiers, he attained the great honor of martyrdom in that very first attack, and his name became immortalized among the great martyrs of Karbala.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى سَلْمانَ بْنِ مُضارِبٍ البَجَلِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Salman ibn Mudharib al-Bajali.
